mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in INDPRO reduces expected manufacturing sector earnings and corporate cash flows, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive average 20-day SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A three-month rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals accelerating labor market deterioration that depresses consumption expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand collapse that amplifies equity downside beyond either single indicator, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously do not produce average negative 20-day SPX returns (mean return >= 0).

mechanism_narrative: During recessions characterized by unemployment above 6 percent, month-over-month declines in the federal funds rate represent emergency monetary easing that lowers discount rates and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; this effect is absent when unemployment is below 5 percent.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE exceeds 6%, FEDFUNDS cuts are followed by negative average 60-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production signal a synchronized contraction across labor and supply chains, generating sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days that exceed the standalone effect of either series.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns are non-negative on average when both PAYEMS and INDPRO decline month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1 million units signals a deep construction recession and durable goods drag that depresses aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are not predominantly negative when HOUST is below 1000 (positive return frequency > 50%).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline exceeding 1 percent signals an acute manufacturing shock that rapidly reduces earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns following month-over-month INDPRO drops exceeding 1% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month cut in the federal funds rate lowers short-term funding costs and triggers a rapid repricing of near-term corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns following month-over-month FEDFUNDS cuts are not positive on average.
